comment_text.iti26:2.1: “A man who eats alone eats only sin” (Rig Veda 10.117.6, Manu 3.118).

root_text.iti26:2.1: “Evañce, bhikkhave, sattā jāneyyuṁ dānasaṁvibhāgassa vipākaṁ yathāhaṁ jānāmi, na adatvā bhuñjeyyuṁ, na ca nesaṁ maccheramalaṁ cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭheyya.
root_text.iti26:2.2: Yopi nesaṁ assa carimo ālopo carimaṁ kabaḷaṁ, tatopi na asaṁvibhajitvā bhuñjeyyuṁ, sace nesaṁ paṭiggāhakā assu.
root_text.iti26:2.3: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, sattā na evaṁ jānanti dānasaṁvibhāgassa vipākaṁ yathāhaṁ jānāmi, tasmā adatvā bhuñjanti, maccheramalañca nesaṁ cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhatī”ti.
root_text.iti26:7.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti26:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti26:2.1: “Mendicants, if sentient beings only knew, as I do, the fruit of giving and sharing, they would not eat without first giving, and the stain of stinginess would not occupy their minds.
translation_text.iti26:2.2: They would not eat without sharing even their last mouthful, their last morsel, so long as there was someone to receive it.
translation_text.iti26:2.3: It is because sentient beings do not know, as I do, the fruit of giving and sharing, that they eat without first giving, and the stain of stinginess occupies their minds.”
translation_text.iti26:3.4: as taught by the great seer!
translation_text.iti26:4.1: Rid of the stain of stinginess,
translation_text.iti26:4.3: they would duly give to the noble ones,
translation_text.iti26:4.4: where a gift is very fruitful.
translation_text.iti26:5.2: to those worthy of a religious donation,
translation_text.iti26:5.3: after passing from the human realm,
translation_text.iti26:6.1: And when they have arrived there in heaven,
translation_text.iti26:6.2: they enjoy all the pleasures they desire.
translation_text.iti26:7.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
